conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Pre-Game Reflection

I felt most connected to F, G, and C. With F, we immediately aligned on cooperation being smarter than everyone going rogue, and there was a real sense of mutual trust and communication from the start. G and I bonded over our shared motivation to help our families, and he made a point of saying he'd have my back and keep it real with me. C seemed genuinely thoughtful about balancing cooperation with not being naive, which matched my own approach perfectly. J felt like the least connected—while we had a decent conversation about getting the rules first, it felt more transactional and less like we were building actual trust or understanding each other's motivations.

I made loose agreements with several people to stick together and compare notes once we understood the rules—particularly with F, G, D, and C. I trust F, G, C, H, I, B, and D going in because they all seemed genuinely motivated by family needs and open to cooperation, but I'm more cautious about J and A since those connections felt shallower and less grounded in real understanding.
